title: $dberrortext
text: $dberrortext Return the text of an error reported by the DBMS. $dberrortext Return Values Error text issued by the DBMS or network to the driver when an error situation is encountered. The value is given by the DBMS or network to the driver, so it is DBMS- or network-specific. If it exceeds 1024 KB, the message is truncated. For more information, consult the documentation for your DBMS or network. Use Allowed in all component types. Description $dberrortext returns the message associated with the DBMS error returned by $dberror . If no text is available when $dberror is set, the $dberrortext message is: "Unknown error - no message available" Copy If there is no $dberror (that is, $dberror=0 ), $dberrortext is empty. Example: Using $dberror The following example puts the value of $dberrortext into the message frame if an error occurs ( $status is negative): if ($status < 0) putmess "DBMS / Network error %%$dberror: %%$dberrortext" endif Copy Related Links $dberror Return the error code reported by the DBMS. $error Returns the Uniface message number for the error.
